title: Per-Workspace Portfolio Adoption Cutover to vector_artefacts
scope:
Cut the portfolio-adoption pipeline over from the legacy mmff_vector.obj_* tables to the per-workspace vector_artefacts substrate. The cutover introduces an explicit scope discriminator on the renamed artefact_field_library table (global / tenant / workspace), a deny-by-default artefact_workspace_fields whitelist, and a master_record_portfolio row per adopted workspace so that BundleView and the adoption-state handler stop reading from mmff_library at request time.

The adoption saga is rewritten to write strategy + work types into artefact_types (scope = strategy or work, with library_layer_id/library_layer_tag provenance), workflows + transitions into flows + flow_transitions, and the model prose into master_record_portfolio. Re-adoption preserves the parent_artefact_id NOT NULL invariant by inserting a placeholder strategy artefact before deleting old strategy rows, then repointing orphaned work artefacts onto the placeholder.

Sanitisation of the legacy obj_* + subscription_* tables is gradual per CLAUDE.md hard rule: each drop is its own follow-up PR triggered by 7 days of zero observed reads after its replacement ships. The five sanitisation cards (S1–S5) are filed on the same plan but tracked as deferred.
value: Closes the residual cross-DB read coupling between tenant pages and mmff_library (BundleView, layer list, field schema, adoption-state) and provides a clean per-workspace scoping substrate for portfolio metadata. Removes the post-reset 404 on the adoption-state endpoint observed today, fixes the animation hang on adoption stream, and gives the team a stable foundation to retire obj_strategy_types_layers, subscription_artifacts, subscription_workflows + transitions, and strategy_layers_adopted in subsequent gradual drops.
implementation_plan: Phase 1 — Migrations (M1-M7): audit, rename + scope, whitelist join, artefact_types extension, master_record_portfolio create, backfill + parity (T1), drop strategy_layers_adopted.
Phase 2 — Backend service + saga rewrites (B1-B8): MasterRecordService, scope resolver, saga steps 2/3+4/5/7, SSE refit, re-adoption flow.

Phase 3 — Backend handlers (B9-B13): GET /api/portfolio/master_record, /api/workspace/:id/portfolio/layers, /api/workspace/:id/fields, /api/portfolio/adoption-state rewrite, workspace deletion hook (deferred).
Phase 4 — Frontend cutover (F1-F5): BundleView swap, adoption-stream assertion test, layer-list URL swap, field-schema URL swap, re-adoption placeholder UX.
Phase 5 — Tests + lint (T1-T7 already woven through earlier phases; T7 standalone lint rule lands with F1+F4).
Phase 6 — Sanitisation drops (S1-S5): filed but deferred per gradual-sanitisation rule; each drop is its own follow-up PR after replacement ships.
areas_impacted: SQL: rename field_library → artefact_field_library, add scope column, create artefact_workspace_fields, extend artefact_types, create master_record_portfolio + trigger, backfill currently-adopted workspaces, drop legacy obj_/subscription_ tables in follow-ups
API: portfolio.MasterRecordService package, scope resolver, adoption saga rewrite, 5 new/rewritten handlers, integration tests for saga + re-adoption + scope matrix + cross-DB canary
UI: BundleView + layer list + field schema URL cutover, adoption-stream assertion test, re-adoption placeholder UX, lint:portfolio-library-read
DEV: lint:portfolio-library-read script + exemption ledger, c_c_v2_workitems_cutover_followups.md sanitisation register entries
DOCS: R047.json living-memory tracking table, c_c_addressables.md/c_c_addressables.md updates if new addressables appear, c_schema.md updates for renamed/added tables

artefact_types extended with workspace_id NOT NULL + library_layer_id + library_layer_tag
master_record_portfolio table per adopted workspace + touch trigger
portfolio.MasterRecordService Go package as sole writer boundary

Re-adoption placeholder pattern preserving parent_artefact_id NOT NULL
Integration tests: saga end-to-end, re-adoption, scope matrix, cross-DB canary
Frontend cutover: BundleView, layer list, field schema URL swaps
5 sanitisation drops (deferred): obj_strategy_types_layers, subscription_workflows + transitions, subscription_artifacts, strategy_layers_adopted, subscription_terminology

lint:portfolio-library-read fails on /api/library/ imports in tenant code paths

risks.risk: Backfill mis-mapping in M4 (workspace_id) or M6 (master_record_portfolio) leaves a workspace either un-adopted-but-marked-adopted or vice versa, corrupting the per-workspace scoping invariant.
risks.mitigation: T1 parity assertion runs inside M6 and fails the migration on count mismatch; T2 audit assertion gates M2 + M4 by ensuring zero NULL-subscription orphans pre-rename; M4 and M6 ship with deterministic backfill scripts that are idempotent on re-run.

risks.risk: Re-adoption (B8) violates the parent_artefact_id NOT NULL invariant if the placeholder strategy artefact insertion or orphan-repoint fails partway, causing FK violations or nullable-parent corruption.
risks.mitigation: B8 is wrapped in a single transaction; T4 integration test seeds a workspace, re-adopts it, and asserts SELECT COUNT(*) FROM artefacts WHERE parent_artefact_id IS NULL = 0; FK constraints surface any mis-step at COMMIT time.

risks.risk: Cross-DB workspace_id has no enforced FK between vector_artefacts.* and mmff_vector.workspaces; orphaned rows can accumulate if a workspace is deleted without B13's deletion hook.
risks.mitigation: T6 nightly canary alerts on any orphan workspace_id; B13 deletion hook is filed as a deferred follow-up but not blocking initial cutover; if the canary fires, the hook is escalated to active.

risks.risk: Stale frontend callers of /api/library/portfolio_templates/:id or /api/subscription/layers persist past F1+F3 and re-introduce cross-DB reads.
risks.mitigation: T7 lint rule fails CI on any /api/library/ import in tenant code paths; F3 includes a grep verification step in its acceptance criteria.

risks.risk: SSE event-name drift between B7 (saga refit) and F2 (frontend assertion) reintroduces the animation hang observed today.
risks.mitigation: F2 assertion test pins down the event-name contract; B7 commit must update the contract together with F2 in the same review window.

risks.risk: Terminology design (12.2) is brainstorm-first and unresolved; S5 drop of subscription_terminology is filed but deferred indefinitely until the new substrate lands.
risks.mitigation: S5 card carries WAITING-ON-12.2 comment; do not drop the table until terminology migration is verified; this is bookkeeping, not a cutover blocker.
